senators.name: James Lankford
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Texas at Austin and M.Div. from Southwestern Baptist Theological Seminary. Career as youth camp director and Baptist minister before entering politics. Theological education and pastoral background. Policy approach driven by faith-based values rather than evidence-based analysis.
education: B.A., University of Texas at Austin
experience: Director, Falls Creek Baptist Conference Center

senators.name: Markwayne Mullin
senators.notNerdReason: Attended Missouri Valley College and Oklahoma State University but did not complete a degree. Career as plumber and business owner (Mullin Plumbing). Cherokee citizen. No academic credentials or evidence-based policy approach. Business background is practical rather than analytical.

OK-01.name: Kevin Hern
OK-01.notNerdReason: B.S. and M.B.A. from Georgia Institute of Technology. Career as McDonald's franchise owner (10 locations) and astronautical engineer at Rockwell International. Georgia Tech engineering and MBA provides strong quantitative foundation. However, legislative approach is more business-conservative than evidence-based.

OK-02.name: Josh Brecheen
OK-02.notNerdReason: B.S. from Oklahoma State University. Career as rancher and state senator. Agricultural background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

OK-03.name: Frank Lucas
OK-03.notNerdReason: B.S. in agricultural economics from Oklahoma State University. Career as farmer and rancher. Dean of Oklahoma delegation. Former Chairman of House Agriculture Committee and now Chairman of House Science, Space, and Technology Committee. Agricultural economics provides some quantitative training. Science Committee chairmanship requires engagement with technical policy but overall approach is traditional conservative.
education: B.S. Agricultural Economics, Oklahoma State University
experience: Chairman, House Science, Space, and Technology Committee

OK-04.name: Tom Cole
OK-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Grinnell College, M.A. from Yale University, and graduate study in British politics at University of Oklahoma. Grinnell + Yale master's represents elite analytical education. Member of the Chickasaw Nation. Former Chairman of House Appropriations Committee — the most fiscally complex committee in Congress. Deep policy expertise built over decades.

OK-05.name: Stephanie Bice
OK-05.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.B.A. from Oklahoma State University. Career in marketing and business consulting. State senator. MBA provides analytical business skills but governance approach not distinctly evidence-based.
